text:
The Paris Olympics have officially kicked off! You can keep up with all of your Olympics content here on KOAA-TV.

Team USA was off to a strong start to the games for the first weekend, bringing in 12 medals throughout the opening weekend of the games.

Those medals all have ties to Olympic City USA as according to the city those medals were won by athletes who have worked and trained in Olympic City USA.



Below you can see which sports Team USA has won medals in and which athletes competed to bring the medals home.

So far, Team USA has dominated in swimming bringing home 28 medals. Of these, eight are gold, 13 are silver, and seven are bronze.

Katie Ledecky - Women's 800M Freestyle

Bobby Finke - Gold, Men's 1500M Freestyle

Katie Ledecky - Gold, Women's 1500M Freestyle

Kate Douglass - Gold, Women's 200M Breaststroke

Torri Huske - Gold, Women's 100 M Butterfly

Jack Alexy, Chris Guiliano, Hunter Armstrong, and Caeleb Dressel - Gold, Men's 4X100M Freestyle Relay

Regan Smith, Lilly King, Gretchen Walsh and Torri Huske - Gold, Women's 4X100 Medley Relay

Ryan Murphy, Gretchen Walsh, Nic Fink, and Torri Huske - Gold, Mixed 4X100 Medley Relay

Torri Huske - Silver, Women's 100M Freestyle

Bobby Finke - Silver, Men's 800M Freestyle

Regan Smith - Silver, Women's 100M Backstroke

Regan Smith - Silver, Women's 200M Backstroke

Nic Fink - Silver,  Men's 100M Breastroke

Gretchen Walsh - Silver,  Women's 100M Butterfly

Regan Smith - Silver, Women's 200M Butterfly

Kate Douglass - Silver, Women's 200M IM

Katie Grimes - Silver,  Women's 400M IM

Simone Manuel, Kate Douglass, Gretchen Walsh, and Torri Huske - Silver, Women's 4X100M Freestyle Relay

Luke Hobson, Carson Foster, Drew Kibler and Kieran Smith - Silver, Men's 4X200M Freestyle Relay

Katie Ledecky, Paige Madden, Claire Weinstein and Erin Gemmell - Silver, Women's 4X200M Freestyle Relay

Ryan Murphy, Nic Fink, Caeleb Dressel and Hunter Armstrong - Silver, Men's 4X100M Medley Relay

Luke Hobson - Bronze, Men's 200M Freestyle

Katie Ledecky - Bronze,  Women's 400M Freestyle

Paige Madden - Bronze, Women's 800M Freestyle

Ryan Murphy - Bronze, Men's 100M Backstroke

Emma Weyant - Bronze, Women's 400M IM

The fencing competition led to an interesting final in the women's individual foil as it was Team USA's Lauren Scruggs up against Team USA's Lee Kiefer. The two battled it out with Lee Kiefer taking the Gold and Scruggs winding up with the Silver.

Lee Kiefer -  Gold, Women's Individual Foil

Lauren Scruggs, Lee Kiefer, Jacqueline Dubrovich and Maia Weintraub - Gold, Women's Team Foil

Lauren Scruggs - Silver, Women's Individual Foil

Nick Itkin - Bronze, Men's Individual Foil

Skateboarding returns for only the second time on the Olympic stage since its debut in Tokyo in 2020.  In the men's street final two Americans took second and third.

Tom Schaar - Silver, Men's Park

Jagger Eaton - Silver, Men's Street

Nyjah Huston - Bronze, Men's Street

Bam Adebayo, Devin Booker, Stephen Curry, Anthony Davis, Kevin Durant, Anthony Edwards, Joel Embiid, Tyrese Haliburton, Jrue Holiday, LeBron James, Jayson Tatum and Derrick White - Gold, Men's Tournament

Napheesa Collier, Kahleah Copper, Chelsea Gray, Brittney Griner, Sabrina Ionescu, Jewell Loyd, Kelsey Plum, Breanna Stewart, Diana Taurasi, Alyssa Thomas, A'ja Wilson and Jackie Young - Gold, Women's Tournament

Justin Best, Nick Mead, Michael Grady and Liam Corrigan - Gold, Men's Four

Clark Dean, Henry Hollingsworth, Nick Rusher, Christian Tabash, Chris Carlson, Peter Chatain, Evan Olson, Pieter Quinton, Rielly Milne - Bronze, Men's Eight

Laura Kraut, Karl Cook, McLain Ward - Silver, Team Jumping

Vincent Hancock - Gold, Men's Skeet

Sagen Maddalena - Silver, Women's Smallbore Rifle, 3 Positions

Austen Smith, Vincent Hancock - Silver, Mixed Team Skeet

Austen Smith - Bronze, Women's Skeet

Brady Ellison - Silver, Men's Individual

Casey Kaufhold, Brady Ellison - Bronze, Mixed Team

In what has been considered a redemption arc for Team USA,  the women's and men's teams both came to Paris with big expectations. Clinching a medal for the first time since 2008, the men's team secured bronze. The women's team came looking to return to the podium and they did, getting revenge and gold after the Tokyo 2020 silver finish.

Simone Biles - Gold, Women's Vault

Simone Biles - Gold, Women's All-Around

Simone Biles, Suni Lee, Jordan Chiles, Jade Carey and Hezly Rivera - Gold, Women's Team Final

Simone Biles - Silver, Women's Floor Exercise

Suni Lee - Bronze, Women's All-Around

Asher Hong, Paul Juda, Brody Malone, Stephen Nedoroscik and Frederick Richard - Bronze, Men's Team Final

Suni Lee - Bronze, Women's Uneven Bars

Jade Carey - Bronze, Women's Vault

Stephen Nedoroscik - Bronze, Men's Pommel Horse

On July 28, Haley Batten's silver medal in mountain biking was the best-ever finish for any American in the event.

Kristen Faulkner - Gold, Women's Road Race

Jennifer Valente - Gold, Track; Women's Omnium

Jennifer Valente, Chloé Dygert, Kristen Faulkner, and Lily Williams - Gold,Track; Women's Team Pursuit

Jordyn Poulter, Avery Skinner, Justine Wong-Orantes, Lauren Carlini, Jordan Larson, Annie Drews, Jordan Thompson, Haleigh Washington, Dana Rettke, Kathryn Plummer, Kelsey Robinson Cook, Chiaka Ogbogu - Silver, Women's Tournament

Matt Anderson, Aaron Russell, Jeff Jendryk, T.J. DeFalco, Micah Christenson, Max Holt, Micah Ma’a, Thomas Jaeschke, Garrett Muagututia, Taylor Averill, David Smith and Erik Shoji- Bronze, Men's Tournament

Olivia Reeves - Gold, Women's 71KG/156LBS

Hampton Morris - Bronze, Men's 61KG/134LBS

Noah Lyles won what looked like a five-way tie in the Men's 100m. Ryan Crouser continued his dominance in Men's Shot Put becoming the only person to be a three-time gold medalist in the event. Sha'Carri Richardson wins silver in her Olympic debut.

Casey Murphy, Alyssa Naeher, Tierna Davidson, Emily Fox, Naomi Girma, Casey Krueger, Jenna Nighswonger, Emily Sonnett, Korbin Albert, Sam Coffey, Lindsey Horan, Rose Lavelle, Crystal Dunn, Trinity Rodman, Jaedyn Shaw, Sophia Smith, Mallory Swanson and Lynn Williams - Gold, Women's Tournament

___



A three-year-old goat named Pongo is making the holidays brighter for families in southern Colorado, one bell ring at a time.
